skill.name: relight
skill.description: Relight still images through the RunComfy CLI by changing lighting direction, color temperature, intensity, and mood while preserving the subject.
skill.summary: Relight product, portrait, and scene images with RunComfy image-editing models.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-crypto alerts are mostly false positives from markdown backticks, model names, prose, and examples. The skill still has legitimate medium risk because it allows RunComfy CLI execution, sends images to external RunComfy services, and documents local token storage.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for controlled external command use. The skill allows Bash(runcomfy *) and shows runcomfy commands that execute cloud image jobs and write outputs. This is intended behavior, but users should review inputs and output paths before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool scope and command examples are explicit. The commands are constrained to runcomfy, so this is medium operational risk rather than confirmed malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: External Image Processing and Uploads
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for network use. The skill sends image inputs and prompts to RunComfy Model API endpoints, which can expose user-provided images to an external service. This behavior matches the skill purpose and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation states that the CLI posts to the Model API and uses RunComfy endpoints. No evidence found of unrelated exfiltration destinations.

medium_findings.title: Local API Token Storage
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for filesystem-sensitive behavior. The skill documents that runcomfy login stores a token under the user configuration directory. The documented 0600 mode reduces exposure, but token handling remains security-relevant.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and permission mode are directly documented. This is a real secret-storage concern, but the file is created by the CLI and not read by this skill text.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for most Ruby or shell backtick alerts. The flagged backticks are markdown formatting, fenced command examples, model identifiers, and prompt examples. No evidence found of Ruby code or shell backtick execution in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible markdown prose and code blocks. The only executable scope is the explicit Bash(runcomfy *) allowance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Service Documentation and Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious hardcoded network indicators. The URLs point to RunComfy documentation, model pages, skill references, and example image placeholders. They are expected for a RunComfy marketplace skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The linked domains align with the declared RunComfy purpose. No evidence found of suspicious unrelated domains.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Lack Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for weak cryptography and reconnaissance. The referenced lines contain metadata, prose, model-selection guidance, and exit-code documentation. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, host probing, or network scanning logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static labels do not match the visible content at these locations. There is no code implementing cryptography or reconnaissance in SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Execution for runcomfy
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for runcomfy commands only. This enables intended CLI operation but requires user trust in the installed RunComfy CLI and command arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash allowlist is explicit and narrow. The risk is real but limited to the named CLI command family.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound RunComfy Model API Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes posting requests to RunComfy Model API endpoints and allowlisting RunComfy domains. User images and prompts may leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is directly documented. It matches the stated image-processing purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential File Location Mentioned
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a token file under the user configuration directory. Secret files require careful handling even when file permissions are restrictive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path and permission mode are stated in the skill. No evidence found that the skill instructs agents to read or exfiltrate the token.

content.value_statement: Lighting changes often require a new shoot or careful editing. This skill routes still images to RunComfy relighting models for controlled results.
actual_capabilities: Routes dedicated relighting requests to Qwen Edit 2509 Relight LoRA.
Uses RunComfy CLI commands to submit still-image editing jobs.
Supports prompts for light direction, color temperature, intensity, and mood.
Suggests fallback edit models for broader image-editing requests.
Documents output directories, exit codes, and RunComfy troubleshooting links.
Provides security notes for tokens, endpoints, and untrusted image URLs.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy account or token.
Processes still images only; it does not relight videos directly.
Image inputs and prompts are sent to external RunComfy services.
Results depend on source image quality and model behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize Product Lighting
use_cases.description: Create a consistent lighting style across catalog images without reshooting every product.

use_cases.title: Change Portrait Mood
use_cases.description: Shift a portrait from flat light to warm, dramatic, or soft directional lighting.

use_cases.title: Prepare Visual Variants
use_cases.description: Generate lighting options for reviews, campaigns, and creative direction decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Relight
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight this still image with soft warm light from the left. Preserve the subject, pose, framing, and main colors.

prompt_templates.title: Product Lighting Match
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight this product image as late-afternoon window light. Keep the product orientation and brand colors unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Portrait Mood Shift
prompt_templates.prompt: Relight this portrait with golden-hour key light from front-left and a subtle rim light from behind. Preserve identity and expression.

prompt_templates.title: Model Routing Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Choose the best RunComfy model for this lighting edit. Explain the routing choice before running the relight job.

output_examples.input: A product photo needs warmer directional light for a lifestyle campaign.
output_examples.output: The skill selects the dedicated relighting route and prepares a prompt for warm window light, soft shadows, and preserved product colors.

output_examples.input: A portrait should look like golden hour while keeping the face recognizable.
output_examples.output: The skill frames the edit around warm front-left light, subtle rim lighting, and explicit identity preservation.

output_examples.input: A broader image edit also needs a lighting change.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a general edit model when relighting is part of a larger composite edit.

best_practices: Use only image URLs or files that the user explicitly provides.
State preservation requirements clearly, including identity, pose, framing, and colors.
Review output paths and account credentials before running CLI commands.
anti_patterns: Do not use private or regulated images unless external processing is approved.
Do not omit lighting direction when precise control matters.
Do not use this skill for video relighting workflows.

faq.question: What does this skill relight?
faq.answer: It relights still images by changing lighting direction, color temperature, intensity, and mood.

faq.question: Does it create images from scratch?
faq.answer: No. It edits existing still images and points generation requests to a different image-generation workflow.

faq.question: Does it require RunComfy?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on the RunComfy CLI and RunComfy model endpoints.

faq.question: Are images processed locally?
faq.answer: No. The skill sends image inputs and prompts to RunComfy services for processing.

faq.question: Can it preserve a person or product identity?
faq.answer: It can request preservation in the prompt, but final results depend on model behavior and source quality.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with warnings. The command, network, and token risks are disclosed and tied to the intended workflow.
